prompt: What happens when a user exceeds their monthly query limit? Provide relevant documentation excerpts including response body and fields.
response:
When a user exceeds their monthly (or otherwise-applicable) usage/quota, the API responds with an HTTP 429 error and a JSON error object explaining the problem. Below are typical documentation-style excerpts and examples you can use in your code to detect and handle quota exceedance.

Example HTTP response (status code 429)
- Status: 429 Too Many Requests
- Body (JSON):
{
  "error": {
    "message": "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details at https://platform.openai.com/account/usage.",
    "type": "insufficient_quota",
    "param": null,
    "code": "insufficient_quota"
  }
}

Common response fields
- error.message (string)
  - Human-readable description of the problem (e.g., instructs the user to check billing/usage).
- error.type (string)
  - Machine-readable category of error (example: "insufficient_quota").
- error.param (nullable)
  - Parameter related to the error (often null for quota errors).
- error.code (string)
  - Specific error code (example: "insufficient_quota").

Possible headers to check
- Retry-After (integer, seconds) — may be present for rate-limit-style responses to indicate when it is safe to retry.
- (Provider-specific rate-limit headers such as X-RateLimit-Remaining or X-RateLimit-Reset may be present in some APIs; check your provider’s docs.)

Notes and recommended handling
- Treat HTTP 429 with code "insufficient_quota" as a hard quota/billing limit — inform the user and direct them to the account/billing usage page or to upgrade their plan.
- For rate-limit (short-term limits) responses you may also see an error type/code like "rate_limit_exceeded" and a Retry-After header; implement exponential backoff and honor Retry-After when present.
- Log the full error.response body and headers so you can distinguish quota problems from transient rate limits or other errors.

If you want, tell me which API provider or endpoint you’re using and I’ll paste the exact documentation excerpt/URL for that service.
